title: Otherworldly Insects
description: A study imagining insect-like forms, sculpted in ZBrush and rendered in Cinema4D with a cyanotype-inspired look.

title: M-Pesa, El Paquete…
description:
Creolization of digital technologies in the Global South and their potential impact on the West.
 
 Full thesis available upon request.

title: Check Out / Check In
description: A point-and-click game where players take on the role of a maid on her first day in a luxury hotel. Each room must be restored by following a sequence of tasks from memory, where order and repetition define the experience. Guided by the overbearing voice of a manager, this experience combines curiosity, frustration and a quiet sense of absurdity in a simple game loop.

title: Rendez-vous 2030
description: Website promoting the UN’s 2030 Sustainable Development Goals for younger audiences. Visitors explore an animated city to learn about each goal, with content focused on the Swiss context.

title: Hello World
description:
Music clip for “Hello World” by Rouge Hotel, unfolding a story through puppet-like animated figures.
In collaboration with Elena Biasi.

title: Cozy Fort
description:
This experience spawns a blanket fort under any table. Participants crawl underneath to explore a space filled with toys and objects reminiscent of childhood.
In collaboration with Elena Biasi.

title: Jupiter 7
description:
Created for the fictional cult “Children of Elara,” this project uses a four-scroll layout where elements snap together to reveal images, buttons, and text. 
In collaboration with Livia Schmid.

title: Liminal Dreams
description:
A journey through abandoned, once-familiar spaces, evoking nostalgia and the lingering presence of childhood memories. 
In collaboration with Emilie Maier

title: Fishtank
description: A short, looping animation following Slimy Stu and his friends swimming through a playful, VR-sculpted environment.

title: Past / Future
description: A short film imagining a barren landscape reclaimed by greenery, where remnants of human activity meet the slow persistence of nature.
